program.name: OpenSIN docs surface refresh control plane

architecture.changeBlockerRule: Do not move docs to another framework without a documented blocker, migration decision, and replacement contract.

repositoryResponsibilities.role: Program control plane and release-order source of truth for the docs surface refresh.
Implements the docs homepage polish, best-practice subpages, and any VitePress theme changes for release v1.1.0.
Bootstraps the reusable design contract and shared design rules for downstream docs work.

issueRegistry.title: [docs surface] Track homepage contract and future visual changes
[Program] Control plane for docs surface refresh v1.1.0
[Homepage] Premium visual refresh inside existing VitePress contract (target v1.1.0)
[Best Practices] Normalize docs subpages, templates, and code surfaces (target v1.1.0)
[Bootstrap] Initialize design contract repo and sync awesome-opensin-design skill (target v0.1.0)

edges.reason: Homepage polish must preserve the existing homepage contract.
The control plane defines ownership, sequencing, and release targets for the program.
Homepage work should follow the documented release order and comment policy.
Best-practice subpages share the same release and dependency policy.
Homepage polish should consume the bootstrap design contract before visual refinement is merged.
Best-practice pages should inherit shared design rules instead of inventing their own.

releases.reason: Initial design contract bootstrap for downstream consumers.
Minor docs release because homepage polish and best-practice subpages add user-facing capability without breaking VitePress contracts.

rules: Patch releases are for copy fixes, broken links, or non-structural styling corrections.
Minor releases are for additive homepage polish, new best-practice subpages, and theme improvements that keep the docs homepage contract intact.
Major releases require a documented contract break, navigation reset, or a proven VitePress blocker that forces a platform-level change.
The surface-plan repo records policy and dependency changes but does not drive the docs runtime version by itself.

mergeOrder.reason: Establish control-plane artifacts before downstream implementation starts.
Bootstrap the design contract used by downstream docs visual work.
Land homepage polish inside the existing VitePress homepage contract.
Land best-practice subpages after shared design decisions are available.

commentPolicy.scope: Non-obvious theme, homepage, and design-contract logic in VitePress docs surfaces.
requiredFor: CSS variables or selectors whose values preserve the homepage contract
hover, focus, motion, or responsive behavior that is intentionally different from default theme behavior
layout guards that prevent homepage and best-practice subpage styles from bleeding into each other
temporary design-contract shims that should later be replaced by awesome-OpenSIN-design artifacts
requiredLocations: adjacent code comment in the theme or style file
linked contract note in docs when the behavior becomes part of the lasting surface contract
PR or changelog note when the logic changes user-visible behavior
forbidden: comments that restate obvious CSS or HTML
undocumented magic numbers for spacing, motion, or breakpoints
silent theme overrides without a contract or release note reference

changelogNotes: Add a machine-readable control plane for the docs surface refresh program.
Lock merge and release sequencing across Surface Plan, OpenSIN-documentation, and awesome-OpenSIN-design.
Require comments for non-obvious VitePress theme and design-contract logic.
